title: AgentTrace
description: A complete record of one agent pipeline execution. Language-agnostic — any language can produce a conforming JSON object and feed it directly to the Origin CLI or the aevyra-witness Python library via AgentTrace.from_dict().

nodes.description: Ordered list of spans in execution order. DAG structure is encoded via parent_id — a span whose parent_id matches another span's id is a child of that span.

ideal.description: The expected / reference output for the run. Optional. Used by ablation's 'ideal' placeholder strategy and by judges that compare against a known-good answer.

metadata.description: Arbitrary key/value metadata for the trace as a whole (e.g. session_id, model_name, pipeline_version, user_id). All values must be JSON-serializable.

TraceNode.title: TraceNode
TraceNode.description: One span in the execution trace — an LLM call, a tool call, a retrieval, or a nested sub-agent invocation.

name.description: Human-readable span name (e.g. 'classify_ticket', 'stripe_lookup'). Does not need to be unique — use id for stable identity.

input.description: The span's input. Any JSON-serializable value: string, object, array, or null.

output.description: The span's output. Any JSON-serializable value: string, object, array, or null.

id.description: Unique identifier for this span within the trace. If omitted or empty, aevyra-witness auto-assigns 'n{index}' (e.g. 'n0', 'n1'). Required when DAG wiring via parent_id is needed.

parent_id.description: id of the parent span. null (or omitted) means this is a root span. Parallel siblings share the same parent_id.

kind.description: What kind of work this span performed. Well-known values are rendered specially by downstream tools; custom strings are allowed.

prompt_id.description: Identity of the underlying prompt. Multiple spans may share a prompt_id when they execute the same prompt at different steps (e.g. a planner prompt at steps 1, 2, 3). Reflex uses this to aggregate blame and optimize the prompt once across all call sites.

step.description: Logical step index in a plan-act loop (0-based or 1-based, your choice). null for simple linear traces.

optimize.description: Mark this span's prompt as a Reflex optimization target. When multiple spans share a prompt_id, set optimize=true on all of them — Reflex updates the prompt once and every span benefits.

tokens.description: LLM tokens consumed by this span (prompt + completion combined). 0 for non-LLM spans.

started_at.description: Unix timestamp (float seconds) when the span began. null if not recorded.

ended_at.description: Unix timestamp (float seconds) when the span ended. null if not recorded.

error.description: Short error or exception message if the span failed. null on success.

metadata.description: Arbitrary per-span key/value metadata. Well-known keys: 'mcp_server' (string — name of the MCP server for tool spans), 'tool_call_id' (string — LLM tool-use id linking this tool span to the reasoning turn that dispatched it), 'error_code' (string — machine-readable error code), 'latency_ms' (number — wall-clock duration when started_at/ended_at aren't available).

nodes.name: classify
nodes.input: I was charged twice — how do I get a refund?

nodes.name: answer

input.question: I was charged twice — how do I get a refund?

nodes.output: You can request a refund within 30 days by contacting support.

examples.ideal: Under our billing policy, duplicate charges are automatically reversed within 3–5 business days. You can also contact support to expedite.
